daggerheart_reference_index.description: JSON structure references for Daggerheart homebrew content creation

environments.json.description: Templates and examples for environment creation
contains: Four environment types (Exploration, Social, Traversal, Event)
Example environments with complete stat blocks

equipment.json.description: Weapon, armor, and item creation structures
contains: Weapon template with damage scaling tables

adversaries.json.description: Adversary creation templates and scaling guidelines
contains: Hit points guidelines and damage allocation
Feature balance and cognitive load considerations

for_agents: Reference these structures when creating homebrew content
Use templates as starting points for new content
Follow scaling guidelines for tier-appropriate balance
Apply design principles consistently across content types
Validate creations against examples and guidelines
for_developers: JSON structures can be imported for content generation tools
Templates provide validation schemas for homebrew content
Examples serve as test cases for balance algorithms
key_principles: Balance narrative focus and dynamic combat
Streamline mechanics and limit cognitive load
Follow official scaling tables and guidelines

questions_to_ask: Does this follow Daggerheart's 7 design principles?
Is the power level appropriate for its tier?
Does it use correct terminology and mechanics?
Will this add to or detract from table fun?
Is the cognitive load manageable for players/GM?
Does it enhance rather than replace existing content?
red_flags: PC features that reference Armor Slots or adversary mechanics
Adversary features that reference Hope or PC-specific mechanics
Weapons with high damage AND powerful positive features
Armor with high protection AND beneficial features
Too many reaction features on single adversary
Features that create positive feedback loops with Fear generation
